assessment_notes: This video is an instructional/tutorial demonstrating the LapSim simulator interface by SurgicalScience. It shows a gallstone-finding exercise with on-screen prompts and visual feedback (yellow circles, green success indicators). This is NOT a standard FLS MISTELS task (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, or Suture tasks). The content demonstrates simulator navigation and camera control instructions rather than performing a procedural surgical skill.
video_classification_rationale: The video contains narrated instructions ('Pull back the left instrument to start the exercise', 'Find the gallstone and zoom in', 'Hold the camera steady until the gallstone disappears'), on-screen text prompts, interactive tutorial elements, and LapSim branding. These are hallmarks of instructional/educational content, not a performance or expert demonstration of an FLS task.
reason_not_scored: This video does not depict any of the five official FLS MISTELS tasks. It is a LapSim simulator training module for laparoscopic camera control and object identification. No FLS rubric applies.

demonstrated_techniques: Zooming and framing techniques for target visualization
Scanning a field for multiple objects
Aligning visual target within a circular reticle
verbal_cues: Pull back the left instrument (camera) to start the exercise
Find the gallstone and zoom in until it matches the circle
Hold the camera steady until the gallstone disappears
Find the next gallstone and zoom in until it matches the circle
common_errors_addressed: Improper camera control (not using left instrument)
Inadequate zoom/framing (object must match the overlay circle)
Unstable camera positioning (required to hold steady)
teaching_content.context: This appears to be a foundational simulator orientation module teaching basic laparoscopic camera manipulation before attempting procedural tasks.
